subject: Can we count on you, Peter?

body:

Please contribute $5 before the deadline to stand with him right now.**](http://action.dickdurbin. name: Andrew
subject: what my grandmother used to say
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Florida Action,
body:
Hey,

Thanks to a surge of support over the last few days, we are only $2,500 away from reaching our new $75,000 goal before our first quarterly deadline at midnight.

I'm grateful for your support today and every day, because our only hope to flip Florida blue and defeat Donald Trump is riding on everyday hardworking folks like you to power this movement.

Here's a link you can use to chip in what you can to help us hit our goal before midnight: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ffa-eoq-deadline](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3564/9e00690/6a93d10d/2407093e/2801514175/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVhOcklIMTkiOiI1In0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ffa-eoq-deadline?amount=5&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1+-+httpssecureactbluecomdonateffaeoqdeadlin&utm_campaign=063019-es-eoq-4&source=063019-es-eoq-4")**

On Friday, Governor Ron DeSantis signed into law an unconscionable bill rolling back Amendment 4 and creating a modern-day poll tax to stop returning citizens from voting.

They're going to do everything they can to stop us from registering and reengaging a million voters, that's the bottom line.

But I have to tell you: after talking with folks across the state and the country this weekend, the continued commitment to our cause has been simply awe-inspiring.

It's because this movement isn't about me, it's about you, our nation, and where we go from here.

We have a choice to make. Do we want to roll up our sleeves and put in the work to move forward, or sit on the sidelines and just hope that things turn out okay?

That's an easy choice for me to make, and I hope it's an easy choice for you.

My grandmother always used to say that if you want to win, you've got to get up off your feet and get in the game. That's why I'm not sitting on the sidelines, I'm laced up and ready to move the ball down the court.

Our future is on the line, and it's ours to change if we put in the work.

Forward.

Andrew Gillum
---

This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com** by Forward Florida Action. name: Tim
subject: counting on you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
Folks,

I know Andrea, Morley, and the rest of the team have been reaching out to you a lot this weekend, so I'll keep it brief: we have just **a couple of hours left** **until tonight 's crucial Federal Election Commission (FEC) deadline -- my first of the election season.**

I know that you have come through for me in the past -- but the stakes have never been higher than they are right now.

| | | | | | Friend,
As we roll into the final month before the election, I wanted to encourage you to watch our stump speech from this past weekend.
Being in northeast Mississippi on Saturday made for a great day on the campaign trail. Our message of the political outsider continues to be music to the ears of conservatives across our state who are fed up with petty politics and career politicians.
As governor, I promise to move the needle on issues like education, infrastructure & making Mississippi the best place to raise a family or hire an employee. Mississippians deserve a fair shake. And I'm here to promise you one.

| | |
# **President Trump Courageously Takes Bold Risk for Peace with Kim Meeting**

“With his meeting Sunday with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un, President Trump showed once again why he is the most transformative figure on the planet,” Richard Manning writes in Fox News.

Following last week’s G20 summit, where President Trump held meetings with world leaders from China, Japan, Russia, and elsewhere, the President “chose to take a chance few would take by inviting Kim to meet him at the Demilitarized Zone separating North and South Korea . . . In Washington – where risk-taking is penalized by a media and political system determined to declare failure – Trump has a different approach.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fed9df933f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

| Critics were too quick to judge President Trump’s North Korea policy after the Vietnam Summit in February, national security expert Fred Fleitz writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=744800d029&e=3ee4985eea). “President Trump’s unorthodox diplomacy has significantly lowered tensions from the dangerous level he inherited from President Obama and opened a door to peace that no one thought possible . . . All Americans should hope President Trump succeeds.”
---
|
---
| “A sizeable majority of U.S. farmers and ranchers continue to approve of how President Donald Trump is handling his job, according to a new monthly poll by Farm Journal,” Bowen Xiao reports for [_The Epoch Times_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c530d0a117&e=3ee4985eea). “Randy Russell of The Russell Group, an agriculture consulting firm, linked the high ratings among farmers to a number of reasons, including Trump’s efforts to counter China’s theft of U.S. intellectual property.” According to Russell, “we’re at a pivotal point, and for the first time, we’ve got a president who’s trying to do something about it.”
---
|
---
| “Following Saturday’s meeting between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping at the G20 summit in Japan, it is clear that Trump’s strategic use of tariffs to end China’s rampant illegal trade cheating and intellectual property theft is putting pressure on the Chinese to negotiate a more balanced trade agreement,” writes former CKE Restaurants CEO Andy Puzder in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c733c23e95&e=3ee4985eea). “It’s about time we had a president willing to stand firm and bargain hard with China to serve our national interest.”
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration deregulation efforts will raise incomes by about $3,100 per household over the next five to 10 years, and sharply reduce prices for consumers, according to a report released Friday by the White House Council of Economic Advisers,” Fred Lucas reports in [_The Daily Signal_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b46a243061&e=3ee4985eea). President Trump’s “aggressive deregulation also cuts consumer prices for prescription drugs, health insurance, and telecommunications.”
---

body:

We just wrapped up our fundraising quarter and I wanted to send a sincere thank you.

When we started our first campaign I knew it would be difficult. I also knew that I was not going to take corporate PAC money and instead would rely on people like you not just to fund this campaign, but to help win. We've got a great campaign team in place, we're meeting with people across Central Virginia and always listening so that we can take the concerns of families in our district and turn them into solutions in Washington.

You've been in our corner each step of the way and for that, I'm very grateful.

Thanks for all that you do!
Abigail

body:
|

Tim is excited to be releasing his plan for education today! As the only candidate who brought up K-12 at the debate, he's made it clear that education is one of his top priorities. His plan reflects his experience as a Congressman and as a father. He understands that in order for our kids to succeed, they must be equipped with the tools necessary to combat stress, trauma, anxiety, and other types of emotional struggle. **Tim is focusing on increasing investment and access to mental health services for children and implementing Social-Emotional Learning --which teaches kids, and adults, the importance of understanding emotions and the skills necessary to manage them.**

**[Tim's plan for education is wide-reaching and ambitious--that's why we need your help. Will you add your name and send a message that you support our vision for education?](http://secure.timryanforamerica. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-edu-upsell?refcode=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190701_1_1_T2&amount=25&recurring=1&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+Timrsquos+plan+for+education+is+widereac&utm_campaign=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190701_1_1_T2&source=TR_EM_PT_ACT_190701_1_1_T2")**

He understands that every child in this country deserves to receive the same high-quality education regardless of their zip code or how much money their parents have in the bank. It is unacceptable that only rich schools get the good stuff when it comes to education--afterschool programs, mental health counselors, highly-trained teachers, healthy lunches, social-emotional learning programs, pathways for family engagement, and new books, technology, and curriculums.

_Tim 's plan would:_
* Put a mental health counselor in every school
* Increase investment in social and emotional learning
* Ensure every child in this country receives the education they need to succeed in the 21st century economy

**There 's no doubt about it, Tim's plan for education gets to the root of the issue: kids need to be socially and mentally well in order to succeed inside and outside of the classroom. **

Thanks for all that you do!
Tim's education team

body:
Thanks to this team, we crossed the finish line of last night's June fundraising sprint in great shape. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771779/691c9bd/6d702e88/11a40b9c/1615642870/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")

| Friend,

Thank you.

**Thanks to this team, we crossed the finish line of last night 's June fundraising sprint in great shape.**

Again and again in my career, I've seen communities come together to accomplish extraordinary things.

I saw it when we poured our sweat, tears, and laughter into building the Wynkoop Brewing Company, revitalizing downtown Denver in the process.

I saw it when we came together to pass universal background checks, insure 95% of Coloradans, and enact the strongest methane regulations in the nation.

I saw it when we launched this campaign with a surge of extraordinary Day 1 support matched by only a handful of other candidates.

**Last night, you came through again.**

I can't wait to see what we can achieve together this summer. Let's find out, shall we?

Giddy up,
John [](https://secure.hickenlooper. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_x_20190701_JH_20190701-x-JH-eoq2ty-all_all_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&zip=34223&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

subject: Watch: The first sitting American President to set foot in North Korea
body:
In a moment for the history books, President Donald J. Trump visited the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) on Sunday... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3df6e903c6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 1, 2019
---
| | |
# **Watch: The first sitting American President to set foot in North Korea**

**In a moment** for the history books, President Donald J. Trump visited the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) on Sunday and met with North Korean Chairman Kim Jong Un. The two shook hands, and moments later President Trump became the first sitting commander-in-chief to ever set foot on the other side of North Korea’s border.

**Watch****:** [President Trump meets Chairman Kim at the DMZ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b6d4024db&e=3ee4985eea)

**“Stepping across that line** was a great honor,” the President said, standing with Chairman Kim and South Korean President Moon Jae-in. “It’s a great day, really, for the world.”

**After their initial greeting,** President Trump and Chairman Kim entered the Panmunjom “truce village” in the DMZ and sat down to revisit negotiations. The two discussed a number of issues, including progress toward President Trump’s goal of full denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula.

**“The two leaders** have just presented such a big hope to the 80,000 Korean people as well as to the whole world,” South Korean President Moon said.

**Photo** : [**“** HISTORIC” step toward peace](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a71c01c15f&e=3ee4985eea)

**North Korea** has confounded American Presidents for decades, as administration after administration stuck to the traditional Washington foreign policy playbook. Meanwhile, tensions escalated while Pyongyang marched ever-closer to developing lethal long-range weapons.

**President Trump** is breaking that dangerous cycle with bold, creative diplomacy—negotiating for peace while backing up his words with a rebuilt, reinvigorated U.S. military.

**“Remember** that it wasn’t too long after President Barack Obama left office that his national security adviser, Susan Rice, wrote in an op-ed published by The New York Times: ‘History shows that we can, if we must, tolerate nuclear weapons in North Korea – the same way we tolerated the far greater threat of thousands of Soviet nuclear weapons during the Cold War,’” Richard Manning reminds readers in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=972fa0323d&e=3ee4985eea).

**“With that comment,** Rice provided a window into the Obama policy perspective toward North Korea, which led to eight years of ignoring and in many respects enabling the threat posed by the communist regime.”

**Today, America and the world** have a clearer path toward peace than ever before.

[Watch President Trump visit our troops at Osan Air Base in South Korea.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=992d998ae3&e=3ee4985eea)

**More** : [Read President Trump’s words to Chairman Kim](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1e30db92b4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: President Trump attends G20 Summit**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=67c01884dd&e=3ee4985eea)

| **At the G20 Summit** last week in Osaka, Japan, President Trump showcased America’s economic miracle as an example to the world. A week after awarding the Presidential Medal of Freedom to [Reagan economist Art Laffer](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8139194194&e=3ee4985eea), President Trump shared how a pro-growth agenda has empowered American workers to reach their full potential.

**“One thing** that every leader—virtually every leader that I dealt with—said is that, ‘Congratulations. It’s incredible what’s happened to the American economy,’” President Trump said.

Trump visited the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) on Sunday... | | | | | The White House • July 1, 2019 | | | Watch: The first sitting American President to set foot in North Korea In a moment for the history books, President Donald J. Trump visited the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) on Sunday and met with North Korean Chairman Kim Jong Un. The two shook hands, and moments later President Trump became the first sitting commander-in-chief to ever set foot on the other side of North Korea’s border. Watch: “Stepping across that line was a great honor,” the President said, standing with Chairman Kim and South Korean President Moon Jae-in. “It’s a great day, really, for the world.” After their initial greeting, President Trump and Chairman Kim entered the Panmunjom “truce village” in the DMZ and sat down to revisit negotiations. The two discussed a number of issues, including progress toward President Trump’s goal of full denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. “The two leaders have just presented such a big hope to the 80,000 Korean people as well as to the whole world,” South Korean President Moon said. Photo : North Korea has confounded American Presidents for decades, as administration after administration stuck to the traditional Washington foreign policy playbook. Meanwhile, tensions escalated while Pyongyang marched ever-closer to developing lethal long-range weapons. President Trump is breaking that dangerous cycle with bold, creative diplomacy—negotiating for peace while backing up his words with a rebuilt, reinvigorated U.S. military. “Remember that it wasn’t too long after President Barack Obama left office that his national security adviser, Susan Rice, wrote in an op-ed published by The New York Times: ‘History shows that we can, if we must, tolerate nuclear weapons in North Korea – the same way we tolerated the far greater threat of thousands of Soviet nuclear weapons during the Cold War,’” Richard Manning reminds readers in . “With that comment, Rice provided a window into the Obama policy perspective toward North Korea, which led to eight years of ignoring and in many respects enabling the threat posed by the communist regime.” Today, America and the world have a clearer path toward peace than ever before.
